title: Prove delegation expiry narrowing is sound
description: The Delegation circuit claims delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry but the constraint enforcement is not visible in the truncated source. Write a Certora-style property spec that proves: for all valid witnesses, delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry AND delegateeExpiry <= currentTimestamp is rejected. Additionally, verify that the comparison uses the range-checked 64-bit values (not raw field elements) to prevent wraparound where a huge field element passes the LessThan comparator. Deliver: a .spec file with 4 invariants and a test harness generating boundary witnesses (expiry = 2^64-1, expiry = 0, expiry = delegatorExpiry ± 1).
